---
title: "Assignment_5"
author: "Jacob_Hillman"
date: "2026-02-22"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
---

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import os
os.getcwd()
# Load dataset (CSV example)
df = pd.read_csv("wdi.csv")

# View first rows
df.head()
```

#Exploratory Data Analysis

##Looking at life expectancy
```{python}
df["life_expectancy"].mean()

```


```{python}
df["life_expectancy"].quantile([0.25, 0.5, 0.75])
```

```{python}
df["health_expenditure_gdp_share"].quantile([0.25, 0.5, 0.75])
```

##Looking at Correlations of Life Expectancy with Other Metrics
```{python}
corr = df["life_expectancy"].corr(df["health_expenditure_gdp_share"])
print("Correlation:", corr)
```

```{python}
corr = df["life_expectancy"].corr(df["education_expenditure_gdp_share"])
print("Correlation:", corr)
```

```{python}

```

## Quantile Plot of Health Expenditure

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Quantile plot showing the distribution of health expenditure as a percentage of GDP across countries. Data source: World Bank WDI."
#| fig-alt: "Quantile plot of health expenditure"

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Ensure numeric and drop missing values
df["health_expenditure_gdp_share"] = pd.to_numeric(df["health_expenditure_gdp_share"], errors="coerce")
health_data = df["health_expenditure_gdp_share"].dropna()

# Sort data and compute quantiles
sorted_health = np.sort(health_data)
quantiles = np.linspace(0, 1, len(sorted_health))

# Plot quantiles
plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))
plt.plot(quantiles, sorted_health, marker='o', linestyle='-', color='green')
plt.title("Quantile Plot of Health Expenditure (% of GDP)")
plt.xlabel("Quantiles")
plt.ylabel("Health Expenditure (% GDP)")
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()
```

---

## Scatter Plot: Life Expectancy vs Health Expenditure

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Scatter plot showing the relationship between life expectancy and health expenditure (% of GDP). Data source: World Bank WDI."
#| fig-alt: "Scatter plot life expectancy vs health expenditure"


# Ensure numeric and drop missing values
df["life_expectancy"] = pd.to_numeric(df["life_expectancy"], errors="coerce")
scatter_df = df.dropna(subset=["life_expectancy", "health_expenditure_gdp_share"])

plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))
plt.scatter(scatter_df["health_expenditure_gdp_share"], scatter_df["life_expectancy"], color='blue', alpha=0.6)
plt.title("Life Expectancy vs Health Expenditure")
plt.xlabel("Health Expenditure (% of GDP)")
plt.ylabel("Life Expectancy (years)")
plt.grid(True)

# Optional: add linear trend line
m, b = np.polyfit(scatter_df["health_expenditure_gdp_share"], scatter_df["life_expectancy"], 1)
plt.plot(scatter_df["health_expenditure_gdp_share"], m*scatter_df["health_expenditure_gdp_share"] + b, color='red', linestyle='--')

plt.show()
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Key summary statistics of selected indicators. Data source: World Bank WDI."
#| fig-label: correlation
import pandas as pd

# Ensure numeric
cols = ["life_expectancy", "health_expenditure_gdp_share", "education_expenditure_gdp_share"]
for col in cols:
    df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors="coerce")

# Compute key statistics
summary_table = pd.DataFrame({
    "Mean": df[cols].mean(),
    "Median": df[cols].median(),
    "Q1 (25%)": df[cols].quantile(0.25),
    "Q3 (75%)": df[cols].quantile(0.75),
    "Min": df[cols].min(),
    "Max": df[cols].max(),
    "Std Dev": df[cols].std()
})

# Optional: round for cleaner display
summary_table = summary_table.round(2)

summary_table
```

As shown in Figure @fig:correlation, the correlation between Health Expenditure as a percent of GDP and Life expectancy is rather low.
